description: A validated, typed manifest with a known schema.
title: Parsed

install.description:
The packages to install in the form of a map from install_id
to package descriptor.

vars.description: Variables that are exported to the shell environment upon activation.

hook.description:
Hooks that are run at various times during the lifecycle of the manifest
in a known shell environment.

profile.description: Profile scripts that are run in the user's shell upon activation.

options.description: Options that control the behavior of the manifest.

ManifestV1.description:
Not meant for writing manifest files, only for reading them.
Modifications should be made using `manifest::raw`.

Vars.description:
The `[vars]` section of a manifest.

Entries preserve the order in which they are defined in the manifest and
are rendered to the activation script in that order, so an entry may
reference entries defined above it.

on-activate.description:
A script that is run at activation time,
in a flox provided bash shell

common.description: When defined, this hook is run by _all_ shells upon activation

bash.description: When defined, this hook is run upon activation in a bash shell

zsh.description: When defined, this hook is run upon activation in a zsh shell

fish.description: When defined, this hook is run upon activation in a fish shell

tcsh.description: When defined, this hook is run upon activation in a tcsh shell

systems.description: A list of systems that each package is resolved for.

allow.description: Options that control what types of packages are allowed.

semver.description: Options that control how semver versions are resolved.

cuda-detection.description: Whether to detect CUDA devices and libs during activation.

activate.description: Options that control the behavior of activations.

unfree.description: Whether to allow packages that are marked as `unfree`

broken.description: Whether to allow packages that are marked as `broken`

licenses.description: A list of license descriptors that are allowed

allow-pre-releases.description: Whether to allow pre-release versions when resolving

Services.description: A map of service names to service definitions.

command.description: The command to run to start the service

is-daemon.description: Whether the service spawns a background process (daemon)

shutdown.description: How to shut down the service

systemd.description: Additional manual config of the systemd service generated for persistent services

systems.description: Systems to allow running the service on

ServiceDescriptor.description: The definition of a service in a manifest

command.description: What command to run to shut down the service

Service.description: Service section configuration with resource limits

Build.description: A map of package ids to package build descriptors

command.description: The command to run to build a package.

runtime-packages.description: Packages from the 'toplevel' group to include in the closure of the build result.

version.description: The version to assign the package.

description.description:
A short description of the package that will appear on FloxHub and in
search results.

license.description: A license to assign to the package in SPDX format.

BuildDescriptor.description: The definition of a package built from within the environment

BuildSandbox.description: The definition of a package built from within the environment

BuildVersion.description: The definition of a package built from within the environment

user.description:
The username or UID which is a platform-specific structure that allows specific control over which user the process run as.
This acts as a default value to use when the value is not specified when creating a container.
For Linux based systems, all of the following are valid: `user`, `uid`, `user:group`, `uid:gid`, `uid:group`, `user:gid`.
If `group`/`gid` is not specified, the default group and supplementary groups of the given `user`/`uid` in `/etc/passwd` and `/etc/group` from the container are applied.
If `group`/`gid` is specified, supplementary groups from the container are ignored.

exposed-ports.description:
A set of ports to expose from a container running this image.
Its keys can be in the format of:
`port/tcp`, `port/udp`, `port` with the default protocol being `tcp` if not specified.
These values act as defaults and are merged with any specified when creating a container.

cmd.description:
Default arguments to the entrypoint of the container.
These values act as defaults and may be replaced by any specified when creating a container.
Flox sets an entrypoint to activate the containerized environment,
and `cmd` is then run inside the activation, similar to
`flox activate -c cmd`.

volumes.description:
A set of directories describing where the process is
likely to write data specific to a container instance.

working-dir.description:
Sets the current working directory of the entrypoint process in the container.
This value acts as a default and may be replaced by a working directory specified when creating a container.

labels.description:
This field contains arbitrary metadata for the container.
This property MUST use the [annotation rules](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/annotations.md#rules).

stop-signal.description: This field contains the system call signal that will be sent to the container to exit. The signal can be a signal name in the format `SIGNAME`, for instance `SIGKILL` or `SIGRTMIN+3`.

ContainerizeConfig.description:
Container config derived from
https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/config.md

Env and Entrypoint are left out since they interfere with our activation implementation
Deprecated and reserved keys are also left out

Include.description: The section where users can declare dependencies on other environments.

dir.description: The directory where the environment is located.

name.description:
A name similar to an install ID that a user could use to specify
the environment on the command line e.g. for upgrades, or in an
error message.

remote.description: The remote environment reference in the form `owner/name`.

name.description:
A name similar to an install ID that a user could use to specify
the environment on the command line e.g. for upgrades, or in an
error message.

IncludeDescriptor.description: The structure for how a user is able to declare a dependency on an environment.

schema-version.description:
Which schema version this manifest adheres to.

Must be a valid Flox CLI version listed in [`KnownSchemaVersion`].

minimum-cli-version.description: The minimum CLI version that can activate this environment.

install.description:
The packages to install in the form of a map from install_id
to package descriptor.

vars.description: Variables that are exported to the shell environment upon activation.

hook.description:
Hooks that are run at various times during the lifecycle of the manifest
in a known shell environment.

profile.description: Profile scripts that are run in the user's shell upon activation.

options.description: Options that control the behavior of the manifest.

ManifestV1_10_0.description:
Not meant for writing manifest files, only for reading them.
Modifications should be made using `manifest::raw`.

Must be a valid Flox CLI version listed in [`KnownSchemaVersion`].

minimum-cli-version.description: The minimum CLI version that can activate this environment.

hook.description:

in a known shell environment.

profile.description: Profile scripts that are run in the user's shell upon activation.

options.description: Options that control the behavior of the manifest.

ManifestV1_11_0.description:
Not meant for writing manifest files, only for reading them.
Modifications should be made using `manifest::raw`.

MinimumCliVersion.description:
The minimum CLI version required to use the environment.

Accepts either a plain version string:
  minimum-cli-version = "1.11.0"

Or an inline table with a reason:
  minimum-cli-version = { version = "1.11.0", reason = "needs feature X" }

schema-version.description:
Which schema version this manifest adheres to.

Must be a valid Flox CLI version listed in [`KnownSchemaVersion`].

minimum-cli-version.description: The minimum CLI version that can activate this environment.

install.description:
The packages to install in the form of a map from install_id
to package descriptor.

vars.description: Variables that are exported to the shell environment upon activation.

hook.description:
Hooks that are run at various times during the lifecycle of the manifest
in a known shell environment.

profile.description: Profile scripts that are run in the user's shell upon activation.

options.description: Options that control the behavior of the manifest.

ManifestV1_12_0.description:
Not meant for writing manifest files, only for reading them.
Modifications should be made using `manifest::raw`.

auto-start.description:
Whether to start all services automatically on `flox activate`.
Can be suppressed with `--no-start-services`.

Services2.description:
Service configuration for V1_12_0: adds optional auto-start behavior
alongside the map of service names to service definitions.

The `service_map` field is a `parsed::common::Services` (BTreeMap tuple
struct) to allow the internal `CommonFields::services()` accessor on
`Parsed` to return a uniform `&common::Services` across all versions.

schema-version.description:
Which schema version this manifest adheres to.

Must be a valid Flox CLI version listed in [`KnownSchemaVersion`].

minimum-cli-version.description: The minimum CLI version that can activate this environment.

install.description:
The packages to install in the form of a map from install_id
to package descriptor.

vars.description: Variables that are exported to the shell environment upon activation.

hook.description:
Hooks that are run at various times during the lifecycle of the manifest
in a known shell environment.

profile.description:
Profile scripts that are run in the user's shell upon activation
(and, optionally, upon deactivation).

options.description: Options that control the behavior of the manifest.

ManifestV1_13_0.description:
Not meant for writing manifest files, only for reading them.
Modifications should be made using `manifest::raw`.

common.description: When defined, this hook is run by _all_ shells upon activation

bash.description: When defined, this hook is run upon activation in a bash shell

zsh.description: When defined, this hook is run upon activation in a zsh shell

fish.description: When defined, this hook is run upon activation in a fish shell

tcsh.description: When defined, this hook is run upon activation in a tcsh shell

deactivate.description:
Per-shell scripts to run when the environment is deactivated.
Mirrors the activation fields above; each is optional.

Profile2.description:
Profile scripts for V1_13_0: adds an optional `deactivate` table holding
per-shell scripts to run when the environment is deactivated. The
activation fields (`common`, `bash`, `zsh`, `fish`, `tcsh`) are the same
as earlier schema versions.

common.description: Run by all shells when the environment is deactivated.

bash.description: Run upon deactivation in a bash shell.

zsh.description: Run upon deactivation in a zsh shell.

fish.description: Run upon deactivation in a fish shell.

tcsh.description: Run upon deactivation in a tcsh shell.

ProfileDeactivate.description:
Deactivation profile scripts. Each field, when defined, is sourced as
the user's environment is being torn down — symmetric to the activation
scripts on the enclosing [`Profile`].

Build2.description:
A map of package ids to package build descriptors.

This is a version-specific copy of `common::Build` because V1_13_0 adds the
`sandbox-allow` field to [BuildDescriptor]; the map is otherwise identical.

command.description: The command to run to build a package.

runtime-packages.description:
Packages from the 'toplevel' group to include in the closure of the
build result.

sandbox-allow.description:
Paths or glob patterns the build may read from outside its closure
without the virtual sandbox warning about them (or, under `enforce`,
blocking them). A leading `~/` is expanded to `$HOME`; `*`/`**` are
matched with `fnmatch`. Only meaningful for the local sandbox modes
(`warn`/`enforce`).

version.description: The version to assign the package.

description.description:
A short description of the package that will appear on FloxHub and in
search results.

license.description: A license to assign to the package in SPDX format.

BuildDescriptor2.description:
The definition of a package built from within the environment.

V1_13_0 adds `sandbox-allow`: a list of paths/globs the build is permitted
to read from outside its closure without a sandbox warning (or, under
`enforce`, without failing). Otherwise identical to `common::BuildDescriptor`.

oneOf.description: Local build; out-of-closure file access is reported with a warning.
Local build; out-of-closure file access is denied (the build fails).

BuildSandbox2.description:
Sandbox mode for a build.

This is a version-specific copy of `common::BuildSandbox` because V1_13_0
adds the local `warn` and `enforce` modes. Keeping the new variants out of
the common enum is what stops older schema versions (whose `BuildDescriptor`
uses `common::BuildSandbox`) from accepting `sandbox = "warn" | "enforce"`.

schema-version.description:
Which schema version this manifest adheres to.

Must be a valid Flox CLI version listed in [`KnownSchemaVersion`].

minimum-cli-version.description: The minimum CLI version that can activate this environment.

install.description:
The packages to install in the form of a map from install_id
to package descriptor.

vars.description: Variables that are exported to the shell environment upon activation.

hook.description:
Hooks that are run at various times during the lifecycle of the manifest
in a known shell environment.

profile.description:
Profile scripts that are run in the user's shell upon activation
(and, optionally, upon deactivation).

options.description: Options that control the behavior of the manifest.

plugins.description:
Free-form data provided by installed plugins, keyed by plugin
package name (`[plugins.<pkg-name>]`). Each plugin defines and
validates the shape of its own table; Flox does not interpret it.
The convention for secrets plugins is a flat table of
`ENV_VAR_NAME = "path/to/secret/in/store"`, read by the plugin's
`profile.d` script at activation.

ManifestV1_14_0.description:
Not meant for writing manifest files, only for reading them.
Modifications should be made using `manifest::raw`.

Plugins.description:
A map of plugin package names to that plugin's free-form manifest data.

Values are opaque JSON: the plugin that owns a given table defines and
validates its own shape, so Flox stores whatever the user writes without
interpreting it.
